name: n8n-code-python
description: Write Python code in n8n Code nodes. Use when writing Python in n8n, using _input/_json/_node syntax, working with standard library, or need to understand Python limitations in n8n Code nodes.
capabilities: Guide users on Python Code node syntax including _input, _json, and _node helpers
Explain data access patterns for processing workflow items
Document available Python standard library modules in n8n
Provide troubleshooting for common Python Code node errors
Compare Python versus JavaScript approaches for n8n workflows
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 124 patterns but all are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation only, not executable code. Markdown code fences (```python) were incorrectly detected as shell backticks. URL references are documentation links. hashlib references show available standard library modules. No actual security risks exist.
valueStatement: Create Python-powered automation workflows in n8n using standard library functions. Learn the correct syntax, data access patterns, and limitations for Python Code nodes.
userTitle: Write Python Code in n8n Code Nodes
limitations: Cannot execute external Python libraries like requests, pandas, or numpy
Does not provide actual code execution - only documentation and guidance
JavaScript is recommended for most n8n use cases due to better integration
Python Code nodes in n8n are beta features with limited helper functions

useCases.targetUser: Automation engineers building n8n workflows who prefer Python syntax
useCases.title: Data Transformation in Automation Workflows
useCases.description: Transform and aggregate data from previous workflow nodes using Python list comprehensions and built-in functions.

useCases.targetUser: Data analysts processing API responses or database queries in n8n
useCases.title: Statistical Analysis of Workflow Data
useCases.description: Calculate mean, median, and standard deviation from workflow data using Python statistics module.

useCases.targetUser: Developers building data quality checks into automation pipelines
useCases.title: Data Validation and Cleaning
useCases.description: Validate incoming data with regex patterns and safe dictionary access using .get() methods.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Python Code Node Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a basic Python Code node in n8n that processes incoming items and adds a timestamp field to each item.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner learning n8n Python Code nodes for the first time

promptTemplates.title: Data Aggregation with Python
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Python code for n8n that calculates the sum and average of an 'amount' field across all input items.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate user needing to aggregate numeric data from multiple items

promptTemplates.title: Regex Pattern Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Create Python code that extracts email addresses from text fields using regex in an n8n Code node.
promptTemplates.scenario: User processing unstructured text data and extracting structured information

promptTemplates.title: Webhook Data Processing with Error Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Write robust Python code for n8n that safely accesses webhook body data with proper error handling for missing fields.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced user building production workflows with proper null safety

bestPractices: Always use .get() for dictionary access to avoid KeyError on missing fields
Return data in the required format: list of dictionaries with 'json' key
Consider JavaScript first - use Python only when standard library functions are needed
Use list comprehensions for efficient filtering and transformation of items
antiPatterns: Attempting to import external libraries like requests or pandas - these will fail with ModuleNotFoundError
Returning a dictionary without wrapping in a list - n8n requires [{"json": ...}] format
Accessing webhook data directly via _json['field'] instead of _json['body']['field']

faq.question: Can I use external Python libraries like requests or pandas in n8n Code nodes?
faq.answer: No, n8n Python Code nodes only support the standard library. For HTTP requests, use an HTTP Request node before the Code node or switch to JavaScript which has $helpers.httpRequest().

faq.question: What is the correct return format for Python Code nodes?
faq.answer: Always return a list of dictionaries where each dictionary has a 'json' key. Example: return [{"json": {"field": "value"}}]

faq.question: Why is my webhook data returning KeyError when I access _json['email']?
faq.answer: Webhook data is nested under the 'body' key. Access it as _json['body']['email'] or use _json.get('body', {}).get('email') for safe access.

faq.question: Should I use Python or JavaScript for n8n Code nodes?
faq.answer: JavaScript is recommended for 95% of use cases because it has full access to n8n helper functions and Luxon date library. Use Python only when you need specific standard library functions.

faq.question: How do I access data from previous nodes in Python Code nodes?
faq.answer: Use _input.all() to get all items, _input.first() for the first item, or _node['NodeName']['json'] to reference a specific node's output.

faq.question: What Python modules are available in n8n Code nodes?
faq.answer: Standard library modules including json, datetime, re, base64, hashlib, urllib.parse, math, random, and statistics are available.
